channel.title: Cell Rep. Phys. Sci.
channel.description: Filtered papers from Cell Rep. Phys. Sci.

item.title: Bio-Inspired Gradient Metamaterials with Exceptional Mechanical Performance
item.description: Lei Yue et al. provide a bio-inspired gradient design strategy derived from gradient microstructures of sunflower stems, creating multi-gradient metamaterials. They break upper limitations of performance of conventional uni-gradient metamaterials and provide insights for the structural design of multi-functional metamaterials with exceptional mechanical performance.

item.title: A Supervised Learning Pipeline for Decoding Nanoparticle Antibacterial Activity
item.description: Chen et al. present an interpretable machine learning pipeline for predicting nanoparticle antibacterial activity. By combining material and bacterial features, the study identifies important design parameters and provides a practical framework for developing more effective antibacterial nanomaterials.

item.title: Microelectrodes Enable Dynamic Biointerfaces for Closed-Loop Organs-On-Chips
item.description: Haoyue et al. review how organs-on-chips (OoCs) with integrated microelectrodes move beyond open-loop sensing toward closed-loop systems that simultaneously monitor, stimulate, and intervene. The proposed closed-loop systems unlock adaptive drug delivery and precision medicine, yet advancing the next generation of microelectrode-integrated closed-loop OoCs demands a holistic, system-level design approach.

item.title: Structurally Defined mRNA-LNPs Formulation Using Automated Inline Monitoring and Hydrodynamically Enhanced System
item.description: Chen et al. present an automated robotic platform that integrates a CFD-optimized microfluidic mixer and an OECT sensor for inline monitoring of mRNA-LNPs formulation. The system produces structurally defined LNPs with higher mRNA payload and fewer empty particles and thus directly enhances in vivo transfection efficiency.

item.title: Mechanically Programmable Metasurface for Ultralow-Power Subterahertz Beam Steering
item.description: Tan et al. show that a mechanically latched metasurface can steer subterahertz beams by changing its surface shape rather than maintaining electrical bias. The low-cost platform holds beam states without holding power, supports multiple beams and beam tracking, and offers an energy-efficient route to directional wireless links.

item.title: Transforming Urban Energy Systems with Scalable Non-Invasive Building Thermal Sensing
item.description: Non-invasive sensing like infrared thermography and ground-penetrating radar offers scalable building thermal diagnostics, but environmental sensitivity and standardization gaps hinder urban deployment. Wei et al. review these challenges and propose integrated solutions to transform isolated tools into city-scale intelligence for low-carbon and climate-resilient planning.

item.title: Data-Driven Molecular Design Rules for Electrolytes in CO2 Electroreduction
item.description: Raj et al. introduce a data-driven framework unifying quantum mechanics, thermodynamic modeling, molecular dynamics, and machine learning to screen 1.3 million molecules for CO2 electroreduction electrolytes. By screening, they identify six promising solvents and derive transferable molecular design rules, consolidated in the open-access COSMIC database for rational electrolyte engineering.

item.title: Self-Powered Piezoelectric Nanocomposite Films for Wearable Health-Signal Monitoring
item.description: Jing et al. fabricate a flexible piezoelectric nanocomposite film based on barium titanate/hydroxyapatite polylactic acid via spin coating for wearable health monitoring. The film sensitively detects diverse human motions with strong application potential.
